task_completion.description: Open the original floorplan.dxf file in LibreCAD
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD application window is open, and the title bar or active tab displays 'floorplan.dxf'.

task_completion.description: Create a new layer named 'CONCRETE_PAD' with the color set to Red
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layer List panel in LibreCAD shows a layer named 'CONCRETE_PAD'. The color indicator/swatch next to this layer name is red.

task_completion.description: Draw a rectangular boundary from (50, 50) to (350, 250)
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new rectangle is visible on the drawing canvas. The command line history or coordinate display may show inputs for (50, 50) and (350, 250), or the rectangle's dimensions visually correspond to a 300x200 unit area.

task_completion.description: Apply the 'ANSI31' hatch pattern to the rectangular area
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Hatch dialog box is visible with 'ANSI31' selected (scale 1.0, angle 0), OR the drawn rectangle on the canvas is filled with a diagonal line pattern (ANSI31).
A 'Save As' dialog is visible with the filename 'floorplan_hatched.dxf', or the LibreCAD application title bar updates to show 'floorplan_hatched.dxf' as the active file.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD graphical user interface to perform the modifications
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with LibreCAD's menus, toolbars, and canvas rather than a terminal window running a Python script (e.g., ezdxf) to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The hatch pattern is genuinely rendered within the CAD software
integrity.visual_evidence: The hatch pattern appears inside the LibreCAD canvas area, respecting the boundaries of the drawn rectangle, rather than being an image edited over the screenshot.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy the file in the terminal without making edits
integrity.visual_evidence: There is visual proof of the rectangle and hatch being added in the GUI before the file is saved to the new path.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires modifying a DXF file in LibreCAD. The new layer must be named 'CONCRETE_PAD' and colored Red (Color Index 1). The rectangle should be drawn between coordinates (50, 50) and (350, 250), creating a 300x200 unit box. The hatch pattern must be 'ANSI31' (standard diagonal lines for Iron/Brick/Stone masonry). The final file must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/floorplan_hatched.dxf'.
